notes_md:
**Tết** is the Lunar New Year, the biggest and most important holiday in Vietnam. It usually refers to **Tết Nguyên Đán**, the traditional new year based on the lunar calendar.

It’s a noun. You’ll often hear **dịp Tết** (the Tết occasion), **ăn Tết** (celebrate Tết), and **đón Tết** (welcome Tết). No classifier is usually needed when speaking generally about it.

Very common alternative meaning: **Tết Tây** = Western New Year (January 1), to contrast with lunar Tết.

Think of “Tết” as more than just New Year’s Day — it’s a whole festive period with family, food, and traditions.
exampleSentences: Gia đình anh thường quây quần bên nhau vào dịp tết.
Chị đã chuẩn bị nhiều món ăn truyền thống cho tết năm nay.
Em rất thích nhận lì xì trong dịp tết cùng mọi người.

definitions.en: The biggest traditional festival in Vietnam, marking the Lunar New Year.
definitions.vi: Lễ hội truyền thống lớn nhất trong năm của người Việt, đánh dấu năm mới âm lịch.

example.en: My family usually gathers during Tet.
example.vi: Gia đình tôi thường sum họp vào dịp Tết.

content.sentence: Tôi sẽ về quê ăn ___ năm nay.

translation.en: I will go home to celebrate the Tet this year.
Everyone is preparing for the Tet coming soon.

content.sentence: Mọi người đang chuẩn bị cho ___ sắp đến.

exampleSentencesTranslated: My family usually gathers together during Lunar New Year.
She prepared many traditional dishes for Lunar New Year this year.
I really like receiving lucky money during Lunar New Year with everyone.
notes:

It’s a noun. You’ll often hear dịp Tết (the Tết occasion), ăn Tết (celebrate Tết), and đón Tết (welcome Tết). No classifier is usually needed when speaking generally about it.

Very common alternative meaning: Tết Tây = Western New Year (January 1), to contrast with lunar Tết.

Think of “Tết” as more than just New Year’s Day — it’s a whole festive period with family, food, and traditions.
